# =============================================================================
# Test fixture: i8 matmul + requantize (DeepLab_int8 dispatch_104)
# =============================================================================

# FILL -> MATMUL -> GENERIC_P_01_1_1_1_01_addi_addi_maxsi_minsi_trunci
# M=1024, N=768, K=192, dtype=i8, out_dtype=i32, final_dtype=i8
# Extra generic inputs: tensor<768xi32>, tensor<768xi32>, tensor<768xi8> (all shape=[N])
DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT = """\
module {
  hal.executable private @main_dispatch_104 {
    hal.executable.variant public @static target(<"llvm-cpu", "static", {}>) {
      hal.executable.export public @main_dispatch_104_matmul_1024x768x192_i8xi8xi32 ordinal(0) layout(#hal.pipeline.layout<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) {
      ^bb0(%arg0: !hal.device):
        %x, %y, %z = flow.dispatch.workgroup_count_from_slice
        hal.return %x, %y, %z : index, index, index
      }
      builtin.module {
        func.func @main_dispatch_104_matmul_1024x768x192_i8xi8xi32() attributes {translation_info = #iree_codegen.translation_info<pipeline = CPUDoubleTilingExpert, {enable_loop_peeling}>} {
          %c0_i32 = arith.constant 0 : i32
          %c-128_i32 = arith.constant -128 : i32
          %c127_i32 = arith.constant 127 : i32
          %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
          %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(0) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags("ReadOnly|Indirect") : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<1024x192xi8>>
          %1 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(ReadOnly) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<192x768xi8>>
          %2 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(ReadOnly) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi32>>
          %3 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(ReadOnly) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi32>>
          %4 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(ReadOnly) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi8>>
          %5 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(2) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(Indirect) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<writeonly:tensor<1024x768xi8>>
          %6 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %0, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [1024, 192], strides = [1, 1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<1024x192xi8>> -> tensor<1024x192xi8>
          %7 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %1, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [192, 768], strides = [1, 1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<192x768xi8>> -> tensor<192x768xi8>
          %8 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %2, offsets = [0], sizes = [768], strides = [1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi32>> -> tensor<768xi32>
          %9 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %3, offsets = [0], sizes = [768], strides = [1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi32>> -> tensor<768xi32>
          %10 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %4, offsets = [0], sizes = [768], strides = [1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<768xi8>> -> tensor<768xi8>
          %11 = tensor.empty() : tensor<1024x768xi8>
          %12 = tensor.empty() : tensor<1024x768xi32>
          %13 = linalg.fill {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[64, 64], [8, 32], [0, 0], [0, 0]]>} ins(%c0_i32 : i32) outs(%12 : tensor<1024x768xi32>) -> tensor<1024x768xi32>
          %14 = linalg.matmul {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[64, 64, 0], [64, 64, 0], [0, 0, 0], [8, 32, 0], [0, 0, 1], [0, 0, 0]]>} ins(%6, %7 : tensor<1024x192xi8>, tensor<192x768xi8>) outs(%13 : tensor<1024x768xi32>) -> tensor<1024x768xi32>
          %15 = linalg.generic {indexing_maps = [affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d0, d1)>, affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d1)>, affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d1)>, affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d1)>, affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d0, d1)>], iterator_types = ["parallel", "parallel"]} ins(%14, %8, %9, %10 : tensor<1024x768xi32>, tensor<768xi32>, tensor<768xi32>, tensor<768xi8>) outs(%11 : tensor<1024x768xi8>) attrs =  {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[64, 64], [8, 32], [0, 0], [0, 0]]>} {
          ^bb0(%in: i32, %in_0: i32, %in_1: i32, %in_2: i8, %out: i8):
            %16 = arith.addi %in, %in_0 : i32
            %17 = tosa.apply_scale %16, %in_1, %in_2 {double_round = true} : (i32, i32, i8) -> i32
            %18 = arith.addi %17, %c-128_i32 : i32
            %19 = arith.maxsi %18, %c-128_i32 : i32
            %20 = arith.minsi %19, %c127_i32 : i32
            %21 = arith.trunci %20 : i32 to i8
            linalg.yield %21 : i8
          } -> tensor<1024x768xi8>
          flow.dispatch.tensor.store %15, %5, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [1024, 768], strides = [1, 1] : tensor<1024x768xi8> -> !flow.dispatch.tensor<writeonly:tensor<1024x768xi8>>
          return
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
"""


# =============================================================================
# Test #2: extract_fused_template — i8 matmul + requantize
# =============================================================================

class TestExtractFusedTemplateI8Requant:

    def test_i8_requant_has_placeholders(self):
        """i8 template should have M, N, K, dtype, out_dtype placeholders."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
        assert '{M}' in template
        assert '{N}' in template
        assert '{K}' in template
        assert '{dtype}' in template
        # i8 matmul has different input (i8) and accumulator (i32) dtypes
        assert '{out_dtype}' in template

    def test_i8_requant_no_concrete_dims(self):
        """Template should not contain original concrete dimensions."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
        # M=1024, N=768, K=192
        assert 'tensor<1024x' not in template
        assert 'tensor<768x' not in template
        assert 'x192x' not in template

    def test_i8_requant_three_extra_inputs(self):
        """i8 requant has 3 extra generic inputs: two i32 + one i8, all shape=[N]."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        _, extra_inputs_spec = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
        assert len(extra_inputs_spec) == 3
        # All map to (d0, d1) -> (d1) meaning shape = [N]
        assert all('d1' in spec['affine_map'] for spec in extra_inputs_spec)
        # First two are i32, third is i8
        assert extra_inputs_spec[0]['dtype'] == 'i32'
        assert extra_inputs_spec[1]['dtype'] == 'i32'
        assert extra_inputs_spec[2]['dtype'] == 'i8'

    def test_i8_requant_preserves_generic_body(self):
        """Template should preserve the requantization arithmetic."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
        assert 'arith.addi' in template
        assert 'tosa.apply_scale' in template
        assert 'arith.maxsi' in template
        assert 'arith.minsi' in template
        assert 'arith.trunci' in template

    def test_i8_requant_no_lowering_config(self):
        """Template should have lowering_config stripped."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
        assert 'lowering_config' not in template

          %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
          %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(0) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags("ReadOnly|Indirect") : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<1024x192xi8>>

          %6 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %0, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [1024, 192], strides = [1, 1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<1024x192xi8>> -> tensor<1024x192xi8>

class TestExtractFusedTemplateI8Requant:

    def test_i8_requant_has_placeholders(self):

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Unit tests for fused matmul template extraction."""

import pytest
import sys
import os

# Add script directory to path for imports
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..', '..', 'script'))

from dataset_generator.mlir_analyzer import HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer


@pytest.fixture
def analyzer():
    """Create analyzer instance without metadata file dependency."""
    return HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")


# =============================================================================
# Test fixtures: real dispatch MLIR excerpts
# =============================================================================

# DenseNet dispatch_179: FILL -> MATMUL -> GENERIC_P_01_01_addf (f32 bias add)
DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD = """\
module {
  hal.executable private @main_dispatch_179 {
    hal.executable.variant public @static target(<"llvm-cpu", "static", {}>) {
      hal.executable.export public @main_dispatch_179_matmul_784x256x512_f32 ordinal(0) layout(#hal.pipeline.layout<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) {
      ^bb0(%arg0: !hal.device):
        %x, %y, %z = flow.dispatch.workgroup_count_from_slice
        hal.return %x, %y, %z : index, index, index
      }
      builtin.module {
        func.func @main_dispatch_179_matmul_784x256x512_f32() attributes {translation_info = #iree_codegen.translation_info<pipeline = CPUDoubleTilingExpert, {enable_loop_peeling}>} {
          %cst = arith.constant 0.000000e+00 : f32
          %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
          %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(0) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags("ReadOnly|Indirect") : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<784x512xf32>>
          %1 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(ReadOnly) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<512x256xf32>>
          %2 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(<bindings = [#hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, "ReadOnly|Indirect">, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, ReadOnly>, #hal.pipeline.binding<storage_buffer, Indirect>], flags = Indirect>) binding(2) alignment(64) offset(%c0) flags(Indirect) : !flow.dispatch.tensor<writeonly:tensor<784x256xf32>>
          %3 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %0, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [784, 512], strides = [1, 1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<784x512xf32>> -> tensor<784x512xf32>
          %4 = flow.dispatch.tensor.load %1, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [512, 256], strides = [1, 1] : !flow.dispatch.tensor<readonly:tensor<512x256xf32>> -> tensor<512x256xf32>
          %5 = tensor.empty() : tensor<784x256xf32>
          %6 = linalg.fill {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[56, 64], [7, 64], [0, 0], [0, 0]]>} ins(%cst : f32) outs(%5 : tensor<784x256xf32>) -> tensor<784x256xf32>
          %7 = linalg.matmul {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[56, 64, 0], [56, 64, 0], [0, 0, 0], [7, 64, 0], [0, 0, 1], [0, 0, 0]]>} ins(%3, %4 : tensor<784x512xf32>, tensor<512x256xf32>) outs(%6 : tensor<784x256xf32>) -> tensor<784x256xf32>
          %8 = linalg.generic {indexing_maps = [affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d0, d1)>, affine_map<(d0, d1) -> (d0, d1)>], iterator_types = ["parallel", "parallel"]} ins(%7 : tensor<784x256xf32>) outs(%5 : tensor<784x256xf32>) attrs =  {lowering_config = #iree_codegen.lowering_config<tile_sizes = [[56, 64], [7, 64], [0, 0], [0, 0]]>} {
          ^bb0(%in: f32, %out: f32):
            %9 = arith.addf %in, %cst : f32
            linalg.yield %9 : f32
          } -> tensor<784x256xf32>
          flow.dispatch.tensor.store %8, %2, offsets = [0, 0], sizes = [784, 256], strides = [1, 1] : tensor<784x256xf32> -> !flow.dispatch.tensor<writeonly:tensor<784x256xf32>>
          return
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
"""


# =============================================================================
# Test #1: extract_fused_template — f32 matmul + bias add
# =============================================================================

class TestExtractFusedTemplate:

    def test_extract_fused_template_returns_template_and_spec(self):
        """extract_fused_template should return (template_str, extra_inputs_spec)."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        result = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        assert isinstance(result, tuple)
        assert len(result) == 2
        template, extra_inputs_spec = result
        assert isinstance(template, str)
        assert isinstance(extra_inputs_spec, list)

    def test_f32_bias_add_template_has_placeholders(self):
        """Template should contain {M}, {N}, {K}, {dtype} placeholders."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        assert '{M}' in template
        assert '{N}' in template
        assert '{K}' in template
        assert '{dtype}' in template

    def test_f32_bias_add_template_no_concrete_dims(self):
        """Template should not contain the original concrete dimensions."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        # 784=M, 256=N, 512=K — none should appear in tensor types
        assert 'tensor<784x' not in template
        assert 'tensor<512x' not in template
        assert 'x256x' not in template

    def test_f32_bias_add_template_has_all_ops(self):
        """Template should contain fill, matmul, and generic ops."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        assert 'linalg.fill' in template
        assert 'linalg.matmul' in template
        assert 'linalg.generic' in template
        assert 'linalg.yield' in template

    def test_f32_bias_add_template_no_lowering_config(self):
        """Template should have lowering_config stripped."""
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        template, _ = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        assert 'lowering_config' not in template

    def test_f32_bias_add_no_extra_inputs(self):
        """This dispatch has no extra generic inputs (generic takes only matmul output).

        The generic's indexing_maps are [identity, identity] with ins(%7) — just the
        matmul result. The %cst used inside the body is a constant, not an extra input.
        """
        analyzer = HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")
        _, extra_inputs_spec = analyzer.extract_fused_template(DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD)
        assert len(extra_inputs_spec) == 0

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
8. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. User asked about fingerprints.json patterns where linalg.matmul fuses with other ops
2. I explored the fingerprints.json file and found 68 unique matmul patterns, 66 of which are fused
3. User asked how to do data augmentation for fused matmul ops
4. I explored the existing augmentation pipeline (augmentor.py, mlir_generator.py, search_space.py, mlir_analyzer.py, etc.)
5. I provided analysis showing that fused op shapes are determined by matmul M,N,K dims
6. User said "ok let's plan the implementation" - entered plan mode
7. I launched explore agents to understand mlir_analyzer.py, mlir_generator.py, and pipeline phases
8. I launched a Plan agent to design the implementation
9. User was asked about scope (how many fused patterns per shape) - selected "Top-N most common" initially
10. User clarified: "same as our current strategy. put all of existing patterns into test set, then 8:1:1 ratio for train and val dataset"
11. User further clarified: "we keep single op MLIR files, but now add fused MLIR files. So we use single op to train MLP atomic and use fused op to train GCN. We use the same shape augmentation as the single op done but just add fused op since you said the following op's shape depends on the major op (matmul)."
12. I confirmed understanding and user said "yes, proceed with the implementation"
13. I wrote plan file at /home/nick/.claude/plans/purrfect-floating-lobster.md
14. I created 7 tasks with dependencies
15. Started TDD implementation - wrote test file, implemented extract_fused_template()
16. All 911 tests pass (905 existing + 6 new)
17. Added i8 requant test cases but haven't run them yet

Key design decisions:
- New `fused_ops` section in patterns.json (alongside named_ops, generic_ops)
- Shape augmentation is SHARED between standalone and fused - one set of augmented shapes
- Standalone matmul MLIR → train MLP atomic
- Fused matmul MLIR → train GCN
- Template extraction: whole-kernel-region approach (Option A)
- Each fused fingerprint treated as its own entry with template + extra_inputs_spec

Important files modified/created:
- /local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/mlir_analyzer.py - added extract_fused_template()
- /local/nick/ai_iree/tests/script/test_fused_template.py - new test file

The most recent work was adding i8 requant test cases to the test file but not yet running them.
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to generate MLIR files for fused matmul patterns (matmul + post-processing generic ops like bias-add, requantization, sigmoid). Currently, 97.7% of matmul dispatches (717/734) in the dataset are fused, but the pipeline only generates standalone matmul MLIR. The user wants:
   - Keep existing single-op MLIR files (for training MLP atomic)
   - Add fused MLIR files (for training GCN that models op interactions when fused)
   - Use the SAME shape augmentation as single op (shared M,N,K shapes) since fused generic op shapes depend on the matmul
   - All 65 unique fused fingerprints get templates, same 8:1:1 augmentation strategy

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Fused dispatch patterns**: `LINALG.FILL -> LINALG.MATMUL -> GENERIC_P_*` where the generic op performs post-processing (bias-add, requantization, sigmoid/SiLU, etc.)
   - **65 unique fused fingerprints** found in dataset0217/files/fingerprints.json across models
   - **Shape determination**: Fused generic op's shapes are fully determined by matmul's M, N, K dims. Extra inputs (bias, scale, zero-point) are 1D vectors of size M or N, derived from affine indexing maps.
   - **Two training targets**: MLP atomic (standalone matmul data) and GCN (fused op data)
   - **Whole-kernel-region template approach (Option A)**: Extract entire fill+matmul+generic from representative dispatch, parameterize with {M}, {N}, {K}, {dtype}, {out_dtype} placeholders
   - **New `fused_ops` section** in patterns.json alongside `named_ops` and `generic_ops`
   - **TDD methodology**: Red → Green → Refactor, per TDD.md and CLAUDE.md
   - **IREE MLIR compilation pipeline**: CPUDoubleTilingExpert, 6-level tiling for matmul
   - **Tile configs shared**: Fused ops use same search space as standalone matmul since lowering_config only goes on the matmul op

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/dataset0217/files/fingerprints.json`** (read-only)
     - Contains all model dispatches grouped by model name, each with fingerprint, dispatch_file
     - 65 unique fused matmul fingerprints, 717 fused matmul dispatches total
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/mlir_analyzer.py`** (MODIFIED)
     - Core class `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer` that extracts fingerprints and templates
     - Added `extract_fused_template()` method after line 250 (after `extract_fingerprint`)
     - Key implementation:
     ```python
     def extract_fused_template(
         self, mlir_text: str
     ) -> Tuple[str, List[Dict[str, str]]]:
         """Extract a parameterized fused template from a dispatch MLIR file."""
         func_body = self._extract_computation_region(mlir_text)
         if not func_body:
             return "", []
         # Step 1: Find linalg.matmul and extract M, K, N, dtype, out_dtype
         matmul_match = re.search(
             r'linalg\.matmul\s*'
             r'(?:\{[^}]*\}\s*)?'
             r'ins\(([^:]+):\s*tensor<(\d+)x(\d+)x(\w+)>\s*,\s*tensor<\d+x(\d+)x\w+>\s*\)\s*'
             r'outs\([^:]+:\s*tensor<\d+x\d+x(\w+)>\s*\)',
             func_body,
         )
         # ... extracts M, K, dtype, N, out_dtype
         # Step 2: Extract kernel lines (tensor.empty through generic close)
         # Step 3: Parse linalg.generic to find extra inputs via affine maps
         # Step 4: Parameterize dimensions in tensor<...> types
         # Step 5: Strip lowering_config attributes
         return template, extra_inputs_spec
     ```
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/tests/script/test_fused_template.py`** (CREATED)
     - New test file with two test fixtures (DISPATCH_F32_BIAS_ADD, DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT)
     - `TestExtractFusedTemplate` class: 6 tests for f32 bias-add case (all passing)
     - `TestExtractFusedTemplateI8Requant` class: 5 tests for i8 requant case (just added, not yet run)
     - Tests verify: placeholders present, no concrete dims, all ops present, no lowering_config, correct extra_inputs_spec
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/augmentor.py`** (read-only)
     - Existing matmul shape augmentation: log-normal sampling centered at 8MB L2 cache
     - `MatMulShape(M, N, K, dtype)` dataclass, `generate_dataset()` for 8:1:1 splits
     - NO changes needed - shapes are shared between standalone and fused
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/compiler.py`** (read-only, planned for modification)
     - `generate_all_mlir_files()` iterates `named_ops` and `generic_ops`
     - `_generate_one_mlir()` inner function: build_placeholder_map → instantiate_template → rebuild_operation_general → wrap_operation_in_module
     - Will need third iteration section for `fused_ops`
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/mlir_generator.py`** (read-only, planned for modification)
     - `build_placeholder_map()`, `instantiate_template()`, `wrap_operation_in_module()`
     - Will need new `wrap_fused_template_in_module()` function
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/shape_source.py`** (read-only, planned for modification)
     - `build_stats_from_shape_source()` reconstructs stats dict from shape file + patterns.json
     - Will need to also populate `fused_ops` from patterns.json
   
   - **`/local/nick/ai_iree/script/dataset_generator/cli/stats_filters.py`** (read-only, planned for modification)
     - `filter_stats_by_op()`, `filter_stats_by_type()`, `assign_pattern_ids()`, `save_patterns_json()`
     - All need to handle new `fused_ops` section
   
   - **`/home/nick/.claude/plans/purrfect-floating-lobster.md`** (plan file)
     - Implementation plan with 8 steps, schema design, file modification list
   
   - **Real dispatch MLIR files examined**:
     - `models/DenseNet/densenet/benchmarks/module_main_dispatch_179_static_benchmark.mlir` - f32 matmul + bias add
     - `models/DeepLab_int8/deeplab-edgetpu-argmax-m/benchmarks/module_main_dispatch_104_static_benchmark.mlir` - i8 matmul + requant (3 extra inputs: i32, i32, i8)
     - `models/Mobile_Vit/Mobile_Vit/benchmarks/module_main_dispatch_10_static_benchmark.mlir` - f32 matmul + bias + SiLU
     - `models/MobileBert_int8/mobilebert/benchmarks/module_main_dispatch_1004_static_benchmark.mlir` - i8 matmul + requant with mixed dim sources

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'get'`**: When first trying to parse fingerprints.json, assumed it was a list but it's a dict keyed by model name. Fixed by iterating `data.items()`.
   - **Test initialization**: Initially used `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer.__new__(HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer)` which skips `__init__` and leaves `compiled_patterns` uninitialized. Fixed by using `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer(metadata_path="/nonexistent")` which gracefully handles missing metadata.
   - **User feedback on scope question**: User rejected the "Top-N most common" answer and clarified that ALL patterns should be included, using the same 8:1:1 strategy as standalone matmul. User specifically corrected: "same as our current strategy. put all of existing patterns into test set, then 8:1:1 ratio for train and val dataset."
   - **User feedback on architecture**: User clarified the two-target training approach: "we keep single op MLIR files, but now add fused MLIR files. So we use single op to train MLP atomic and use fused op to train GCN."

5. Problem Solving:
   - Determined that shape augmentation doesn't need to change (fused op shapes derived from matmul M,N,K)
   - Chose whole-kernel-region template approach over decomposed composition approach to avoid SSA threading complexity
   - Designed `extra_inputs_spec` to capture affine map and dtype for each extra generic input
   - Successfully implemented `extract_fused_template()` that handles f32 case (6 passing tests)
   - Added i8 requant test cases (5 tests, not yet run)

6. All user messages:
   - "dataset0217/files/fingerprints.json tell me the pattern that linalg.matmul op fuses with other computation op"
   - "good. Now, I would like to generate MLIR files with those patterns. We already have matmul augmentation for single matmul op. Tell me how to do data augmentation for multiple op (focus on matmul + fused ops now)"
   - "ok let's plan the implementation"
   - "ask me again" (after interrupted question)
   - "same as our current strategy. put all of existing patterns into test set, then 8:1:1 ratio for train and val dataset. understand?"
   - "no ~ the last question   how many fused patterns per shape?"
   - "ask me your question again" (after rejected question)
   - (Clarified the scope question - rejected, wanted to provide additional context)
   - "we keep single op MLIR files, but now add fused MLIR files. So we use single op to train MLP atomic and use fused op to train GCN. We use the same shape agumentation as the single op done but just add fused op since you said the following op's shape depends on the major op (matmul). show me your understanding"
   - "yes, proceed with the implementation"
   - "Your task is to create a detailed summary..." (current message)

7. Pending Tasks (from TaskList):
   - **#1 [completed]**: Extract kernel region helper in mlir_analyzer.py
   - **#2 [in_progress]**: Implement extract_fused_template() in mlir_analyzer.py - f32 case done (6 tests pass), i8 requant tests just added but NOT YET RUN
   - **#3 [pending, blocked by #2]**: Populate fused_ops in collect_operation_statistics()
   - **#4 [pending, blocked by #3]**: Update stats_filters.py for fused_ops
   - **#5 [pending, blocked by #2]**: Implement wrap_fused_template_in_module() in mlir_generator.py
   - **#6 [pending, blocked by #4, #5]**: Handle fused_ops in generate_all_mlir_files()
   - **#7 [pending, blocked by #6]**: End-to-end integration test

8. Current Work:
   Working on Task #2 (extract_fused_template). The f32 bias-add case is complete and passing (6 tests). I just added 5 new test cases for the i8 requant pattern (`TestExtractFusedTemplateI8Requant`) to `tests/script/test_fused_template.py` but have NOT yet run them. These tests verify:
   - `test_i8_requant_has_placeholders` - {M}, {N}, {K}, {dtype}, {out_dtype}
   - `test_i8_requant_no_concrete_dims` - no M=1024, N=768, K=192
   - `test_i8_requant_three_extra_inputs` - 3 extra inputs (i32, i32, i8) all shape=[N]
   - `test_i8_requant_preserves_generic_body` - arith.addi, tosa.apply_scale, etc.
   - `test_i8_requant_no_lowering_config`

   Current test status: 911 passed (905 existing + 6 new f32 tests). The i8 tests are written but not executed.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Run the newly added i8 requant tests with `pytest tests/script/test_fused_template.py -x -v` to see if they pass (GREEN) or need implementation fixes (RED→GREEN cycle). This is directly continuing the TDD work on Task #2.

   The last action was editing the test file to add `DISPATCH_I8_REQUANT` fixture and `TestExtractFusedTemplateI8Requant` class. The user's last explicit request before the summary was "yes, proceed with the implementation."
</summary>
